definitions: A campaign directed against a person or group accused of disloyalty, subversion, or other real or imagined crimes, typically with the use of unfair investigatory and underhanded techniques.
A search for and persecution of people accused of witchcraft.
examples: The senator claimed the investigation was a political witch hunt.
Historically, witch hunts were common in some parts of Europe.
She felt like she was the target of a witch hunt at work.
